title: The One With the Stoned Guy

lines.text: Central Perk, Rachel is serving Joey, Ross, and Monica their drinks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Joey) Coffee. (Hands it to him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross) Cappuccino. (Hands it to him.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Grazie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And a nice hot cider for Monica. (Hands it to her.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aww, thank you. (Notices something.) Uh Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why does my cinamon stick have an eraser?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! That's why. (Rachel checks behind her ear, and finds a cinamon stick.) I'm sorry!
She takes the pencil out of Monica's coffee and Monica puts her cup down in disgust.
Opening Credits
Chandler's job, Chandler is typing data into his computer, he keeps typing even while taking a drink of coffee with one hand. One of his co-workers walks by.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mrs. Tedlock. You're looking lovely today. And may I say, that is a very flattering sleeve length on you.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Tedlock
lines.text: Yes. Well, Mr. Kostelick wants you to stop by his office at the end of the day.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, listen. If this is about those prank memos, I had nothing to do with them. Really. Nothing at all. Really. (Chandler tries to hide a rubber chicken from the woman.) Nothing.
Central Perk, everyone is there but Chandler. Phoebe runs in, excitedly.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys! Chandler's coming and he says he has, like, this incredible news, so when he gets here, we could all act like, you know...
Chandler comes in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Never mind. But it was going to be really good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, it's a typical day at work. I'm inputting my numbers, and big Al calls me into his office and tells me he wants to make me processing supervisor.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: That's great!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So.... I quit.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why? This was supposed to be a temp job!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, Chandler... you've been there for five years.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If I took this promotion, it'd be like admitting that this is what I actually do.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So was it a lot more money?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It doesn't matter. I just don't want to be one of those guys that's in his office until twelve o'clock at night worrying about the WENUS.
Everyone looks at him, confused.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ... the WENUS?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Weekly Estimated Net Usage Systems. A processing term.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sarcastic) Oh. That WENUS.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what're you going to do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know. That's the thing. I don't know what I want to do. I just know I'm not going to figure it out working there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oooh! I have something you can do! I have this new massage client... Steve? (pause) Anyway, he's opening up a restaurant and he's looking for a head chef.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (taps Phoebe on her shoulder) Um... hi there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi! (turns back to Chandler, then to Monica) Oh, yeah, no, I know. You're a chef. I know, and I thought of you first, but um, Chandler's the one who needs a job right now, so....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah... I just don't have that much cheffing experience. Unless it's an all-toast restaurant.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Monica's tapping) Yeah, yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, what kind of food is he looking for?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he wants to do some ecclectic, so he's looking for someone who can, you know, create the entire menu.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (excited) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know! (turns to Chandler) So, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks, Phoebe. But I just don't really see myself in a big white hat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK. (pause) Oh Monica! Guess what!
Monica and Rachel's, Chandler walks in, wearing a suit.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can you see my nipples through this shirt?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. But don't worry, I'm sure they're still there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where are you going, Mr. Suity-Man?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I have an appointment to see Dr. Robert Pillman, career counselor a-gogo. (pause) I added the "a-gogo."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Career counselor?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you guys all know what you want to do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you guys in the living room all know what you want to do. You know, you have goals. You have dreams. I don't have a dream.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, the lesser-known "I don't have a dream" speech.
Monica enters, excited.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I love my life, I love my life!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! Brian's Song!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: The meeting with the guy went great?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So great! He showed me where the restaurant's going to be. It's this, it's this cute little place on 10th Street. Not too big, not too small. Just right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Was it formerly owned by a blonde woman and some bears?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So anyway, I'm cooking dinner for him Monday night. You know, kind of like an audition. And Phoebe, he really wants you to be here, which will be great for me because then you can 'ooh' and 'ahh' and make yummy noises.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you going to make?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (as though Rachel wasn't paying attention) Yummy noises.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (pause) And Monica, what are you going to make?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know. I don't know. It's just going to be so great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! I know what you could make! (runs over to join Monica and Rachel in the kitchen) I know! Oh, you should definitely make that thing... you know, with the stuff? (Monica doesn't know.) You know, that thing... with the stuff...? OK, I don't know. (sits down)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey guys, does anybody know a good date place in the neighborhood?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How about Tony's? If you can finish a 32-ounce steak, it's free.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK, ahem, hey, does anybody know a good place if you're not dating a puma?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who are you going out with?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, is this the bug lady?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (trying to sound like a bug) Bzzzz.... I love you, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Her name is Celia. She's not a bug lady. She's curator of insects at the museum.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So what are you guys going to do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I just thought we could go out to dinner, and then maybe bring her back to my place and I'd introduce her to my monkey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And he's not speaking metaphorically.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (aside to Ross) So.... back to your place...you thinking, maybe... (gestures with hands, back and forth) huh-huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I don't know.... (gestures) huh-huh.... but I'm hoping (gestures) huh-huh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm telling you, that monkey is a chick magnet! She's going to take one look at his furry, cute little face and it'll seal the deal.
Ross's apartment, Marcel is hanging from Celia's hair, and she is screaming, trying to get him off.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Celia, don't worry!  Don't scream!  He's not going to hurt you! Soothing tones, Celia. Soothing tones! Marcel...

lines.speaker: Celia
lines.text: I can't stand this! He's got his claws in my...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright... (lifts Marcel away)
Monica and Rachel's, everyone is there but Ross and Chandler. Monica is making food, and having everyone try it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Joey) OK, try this salmon mousse.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (tasting) Mmmm. Good.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is it better than the other salmon mousse?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's creamier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, well, is that better?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know. We're talking about whipped fish, Monica. I'm just happy I'm keeping it down, y'know?
Chandler kicks the door closed, angrily. His clothes are askew, he looks beat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My God! What happened to you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eight and a half hours of aptitude tests, intelligence tests, personality tests... and what do I learn? (he taps the results and reads them) "You are ideally suited for a career in data processing for a large multinational corporation."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's so great! 'Cause you already know how to do that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can you believe it? I mean, don't I seem like somebody who should be doing something really cool? You know, I just always pictured myself doing something...something.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (comes up and rubs him on the chest) Oh Chandler, I know, I know... oh, hey! You can see your nipples through this shirt!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (brings a plate of tiny appetizers over) Here you go, maybe this'll cheer you up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh, you know, I had a grape about five hours ago, so I'd better split this with you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's supposed to be that small. It's a pre-appetizer. The French call it an amouz-bouche.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (tastes it) Well.... it is amouz-ing...
Phone rings. Monica answers it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (on phone) Hello? (Listens) Oh, hi Wendy! (Listens) Yeah, eight o'clock. (Listens) What did we say? Ten dollars an hour?... (Listens) OK, great. (Listens) All right, I'll see you then. Bye. (hangs up)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ten dollars an hour for what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I asked one of the waitresses at work if she'd help me out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (hurt) Waitressing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well... of course I thought of you! But... but...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But, but?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But, you see, it's just... this night has to go just perfect, you know? And, well, Wendy's more of a... professional waitress.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! I see. And I've sort of been maintaining my amateur status so that I can waitress in the Olympics.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I don't mean to brag, but I waited tables at Innsbruck in '76. (dead silence) Amouz-bouche? (holds out tray)
Ross' apartment, Girl, You'll Be A Woman Soon (the original, not that cruddy Urge Overkill version) is playing. Ross and Celia are kissing passionately.

lines.speaker: Celia
lines.text: Talk to me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK.... um, a weird thing happened to me on the train this morning...

lines.speaker: Celia
lines.text: No no no. Talk... dirty.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (embarrassed) Wha... what, here?

lines.speaker: Celia
lines.text: Yes...

lines.speaker: Celia
lines.text: Say something..... hot.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (panicked) Er.... um.....

lines.speaker: Celia
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um... uh.... vulva.
Commercial Break
Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Ross are there, discussing what happened last night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (in disbelief) Vulva?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Alright, I panicked, alright? She took me by surprise. You know, but it wasn't a total loss. I mean, we ended up cuddling.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sarcastic) Whoaa!! You cuddled? How many times??

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Shut up! It was nice. I just... I don't think I'm the dirty-talking kind of guy, you know?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's the big deal? You just say what you want to do to her. Or what you want her to do to you. Or what you think other people might be doing to each other. I'll tell you what. Just try something on me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (deadpan) Please be kidding.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not? Come on! Just, just close your eyes and tell me what you'd like to be doing right now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK. (closes eyes) I'm in my apartment...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: ....yeah... what else?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's it. I'm in my apartment, you're not there, we're not having this conversation. (gets up, walks across room)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (walks to catch up to him) Alright, look, I'll start, OK?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, please.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on. Come on. Alright, ready, look! (in a low voice) Oh... Ross.... you get me so hot. I want your lips on me now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (impressed) Wow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, now you say something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I... ahem... I really don't think so.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! You like this woman, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You want to see her again, right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well if you can't talk dirty to me, how're you going to talk dirty to her? Now tell me you want to caress my butt!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK, turn around. (Joey looks taken aback) I just don't want you staring at me when I'm doing this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (turning around) Alright, alright. I'm around. Go ahead.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahem... I want.... OK, I want to... feel your... hot, soft skin with my lips.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There you go! Keep going. Keep going!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I, er...
At this point, Chandler walks into the living room from his bedroom. Ross and Joey both have their backs to him, so they don't notice. Chandler sees the situation and remains quiet, watching.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I want to take my tongue... and...
Chandler is completely astounded.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Say it... say it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...run it all over your body until you're... trembling with... with...
Chandler leans back against the wall and Ross and Joey hear him. Ross and Joey both notice at the same time. They slowly stop, and then very slowly turn around to see Chandler staring at them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (smiling)....with??

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (rushing to explain) Funny story!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're not going to believe this!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's OK. It's OK. I was always rooting for you two kids to get together.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Chandler, while you were sleeping that guy from your old job called again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Again?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And again, and again, and again... (phone rings, he answers) Hello? (hands phone to Chandler) And again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (on phone) Hey Mr. Kostelic! How's life on the fifteenth floor? (Listens) Yeah, I miss you too. (Listens) Yeah, it's a lot less satisfying to steal pens from your own home, you know? (Listens) Well, that's very generous (Listens) er, but look, this isn't about the money. I need something that's more than a job. I need something I can really care about.... (Listens) And that's on top of the yearly bonus structure you mentioned earlier? (Listens) Look, Al, Al... I'm not playing hardball here, OK? This is not a negotiation, this is a rejection! (Listens) No! No! No, stop saying numbers! I'm telling you, you've got the wrong guy! You've got the wrong guy! (Listens) I'll see you on Monday! (slams the phone down)
Chandler's new window office, he is showing Phoebe around.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (excited) Wow! It's huge! It's so much bigger than the cubicle. Oh, this is a cube.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look at this! (he opens the curtain to a view of New York City)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! You have a window!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes indeedy! (they look outside) With a beautiful view of...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh look! That guy's peeing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (walks away from window) OK, that's enough of the view. Check this out, look at this. Sit down, sit down.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sitting) OK.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is great! (he presses a button on his intercom) Helen, could you come in here for a moment?
An unamused woman walks into the office.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you Helen, that'll be all.
She leaves, obviously perturbed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Last time I do that, I promise.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is on the phone. Rachel walks in and overhears the conversation.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shouting on phone) Wendy, we had a deal! (Listens) Yeah, you promised! Wendy! Wendy! Wendy! (hangs up)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who was that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wendy bailed. I have no waitress.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh... that's too bad. Bye bye. (she walks away towards the door)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ten dollars an hour.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Twelve dollars an hour.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mon. I wish I could, but I've made plans to walk around.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know, Rachel, when you ran out of your wedding, I was there for you. I put a roof over your head, and if that means nothing to you... (Rachel isn't buying it, desperate) twenty dollars an hour.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Done.
Monica and Rachel's, later. Rachel is waitressing, Monica is cooking. Phoebe walks in with Steve (Crystal Duck winner Jon Lovitz).

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well hello! Welcome to Monica's. May I take your coat?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi Steve!

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Hello, Monica. (to Rachel) Hello, greeter girl.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Steve) This is Rachel.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: (unconcerned) Yeah, OK.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (overemphasizing) Mmmmmm! Everything smells so delicious! You know, I can't remember a time I smelt such a delicious combination of (Monica signals her to stop) of, OK, smells.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: It's a lovely apartment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, thank you. Would you like a tour?

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: I was just being polite, but, alright.
They leave on the tour and Rachel goes to follow them but Phoebe stops her and drags her into the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's up?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (whispers) In the cab, on the way over, Steve blazed up a doobie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Smoked a joint? You know, lit a bone? Weed? Hemp? Ganja?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, OK. I'm with you, Cheech. OK.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: (from the living room) Is it dry in here? (licks his lips)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let me, let me get you some wine!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I think we're ready for our first course. (Steve sits, Monica brings over a tray) OK, um, these are rot-shrimp ravioli, and celantro pondou sauce... (Steve starts to eat them one by one, quickly)... with just a touch of mints... and... (he finishes)... ginger.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Well, smack my ass and call me Judy! These are fantastic!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm so glad you liked them!

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Like 'em? I could eat a hundred of them!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, well... um, that's all there are of these. But in about eight and a half minutes, we'll be serving some delicious onion tartlets.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Tartlets. Tartlets. Tartlets. The word has lost all meaning. (he gets up and goes into the kitchen)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me? Can I help you with anything?

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: You know, I don't know what I'm looking for.

Rachel tries to get Monica's attention to tell her Steve is stoned. She pretends to drag on a joint, and Monica thinks she's giving her the 'OK' signal. Then Rachel does it again, inhaling deeply this time. Monica waves it off as though she doesn't believe it.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: (from kitchen) Ah, cool! Taco shells! (Rachel motions, "You see!") You know, these are... they're like a little corn envelope.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (joining him and taking the taco shells) You know that? You don't want to spoil your appetite.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: (looking in cabinets) Hey! Sugar-O's! (grabs the cereal box)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know, if you just wait another... six and a half minutes...

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Macaroni and cheese! We gotta make this!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, we don't. (reaches for box)

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Oh, OK. (he drops the box on the floor) Oh, sorry. (When she bends down to pick it up he grabs a package of Gummi-bears from the cabinet.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why don't you just have a seat here? (he sits at the table, then tries to secretly eat the Gummi-bears. Monica spots him.) OK... give me the Gummi-bears.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: (childishly) No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Give them to me.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Alright, we'll share.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, give me the...

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Well then you can't have any. (she grabs for the package, and it breaks open. Gummi-bears fly everywhere, some into the punch bowl on the table.) Bear overboard! I think he's drowning. (he throws some Sugar-O's into the punch bowl) Hey fellows! Grab on a Sugar-O... save yourself! (Mimicking the bears) "Help!  I'm drowning!   Help!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (furious) That's it! Dinner is over!

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why? It's just that I've waited seven years for an opportunity like this, and you can't even wait four and a half minutes for a stupid onion tartlet?
The oven goes off.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: (excited) Hey!
Central Perk, all are there except Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What a tool!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You don't want to work for a guy like that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know... it's just... I thought this was, you know... it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, you'll get there. You're an amazing chef.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! You know all those yummy noises? I wasn't faking.
Ross gets up and goes over to the counter and Joey follows him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Ross) So, er... how did it go with Celia?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I was unbelievable.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was the James Michener of dirty talk. It was the most elaborate filth you have ever heard. I mean, there were characters, plot lines, themes, a motif... at one point there were villagers.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa! And the... (gestures with hands) huh-huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, ahem... you know, by the time we'd finished with all the dirty talk, it was kinda late... and we were both kind of exhausted, so uh...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You cuddled.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, which was nice.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys wanna try and catch a late movie or something?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Maybe, but shouldn't we wait for Chandler?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, where the hell is he?
Chandler's office, he's on the phone, agitated.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (on phone) Yes, Fran. I know what time it is, but I'm looking at the WENUS and I'm not happy!... (Listens) Oh, really, really, really? Well, let me tell you something... you will care about it, because I care about it! You got it? Good! (slams phone down, then leans back and realizes what just happened) Whooooaaaa....
Closing Credits
Phoebe's massage parlour, she has Steve on the table, and is giving him an extra-painful massage.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How's this? (presses down hard)

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Eeeee!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sorry. How about over here? (presses down hard again)

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Aaaaah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See, that just means it's working. Does this hurt? (presses down elsewhere)

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What about this? (she starts using her elbows on his back, he yells in pain)

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Aaaaahhh!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There you go! (She continues to work him over with her elbows and he continues to yell in pain.)
End
